title: Lock target IA and routing model

title: Design Readiness theme
description: Design the new Readiness theme around existing scan data. It should answer whether the project is ready to use Fabric and provide recommended CLI next steps without executing commands.
test: Check design against getScan ScanReport fields; verify no POST/command execution; verify empty/error/loading states are specified.
acceptance_criteria: Readiness includes scan summary, framework evidence, README/CONTRIBUTING readiness, existing Fabric status, file/ignored counts, recommendations, and CLI next-step copy. It remains read-only.
hints: Use existing getScan() and /api/scan. Do not design init/update buttons that execute actions. || packages/dashboard/src/api/client.ts;packages/server/src/api/scan.ts
execution_directives: planning only; later implementation should add tests for loading/error/scan report states
findings: Readiness should be first new UI surface and can be implemented without backend changes unless scan service convergence is required separately.

title: Design Rules Explain theme
description: Design Rules Explain as the home for current Rule Topology and Rules Tree, plus future semantic/path hit explanation. Treat human_locked_nearby as legacy/future extension, not a promised active capability.
test: Check design maps topology and rules tree under same theme; verifies /api/rules/context is the explain data source; confirms no rule/registry edit affordances.
acceptance_criteria: Rules Explain shows rule topology, registry/tree detail, path input, L0/L1/L2 hit reasons, coverage/dependencies, revision metadata. It must not expose rule CRUD or registry editing.
hints: RulesTree currently has empty lockFiles; do not design active lock indicators unless new data source is added. || packages/dashboard/src/views/rule-topology.tsx;packages/dashboard/src/views/rules-tree.tsx
execution_directives: planning only; later implementation should keep getRules/getRulesContext read-only
findings: Rules Explain is the strongest existing capability; design should reduce duplicate navigation between topology and rules tree.
acceptance_met: Rules Explain data model and exclusions specified.

title: Design Timeline theme
description: Design Timeline as the home for Intent Timeline, human annotation, History Replay, and future ledger analysis. Annotation should be described as audit context, not approval.
test: Check design maps intent timeline and history replay under Timeline; verify annotateIntent remains the only write interaction and is framed as audit annotation.
acceptance_criteria: Timeline includes AI/human ledger filters, annotation, chronological evidence chain, history replay by ledger point, and optional future event/legacy ledger status. No Approval language.
hints: Annotation is still a POST but should remain narrow and audit-focused. || packages/dashboard/src/views/intent-timeline.tsx;packages/dashboard/src/views/history-replay.tsx;packages/dashboard/src/api/client.ts
execution_directives: planning only; later implementation should test annotation and history state flows
findings: Timeline consolidates ledger and replay while avoiding resurrecting old Approval semantics.
acceptance_met: Timeline scope and annotation framing specified.

title: Design Health theme with Runtime & Boundaries
description: Design Health as Doctor plus forensic/audit, runtime status, and tooling boundary. Health may show fixable/manual/warning issues and recommended CLI commands, but must not execute doctor --fix or other repair actions.
test: Check design against DoctorReport fields; verify no fix POST/API; verify runtime/boundary content explains CLI vs MCP vs Web responsibilities.
acceptance_criteria: Health includes Doctor status, issue categories, target files, meta/computed revision, event ledger path, runtime/SSE/MCP visibility, and tooling boundary guidance. Repair actions are CLI suggestions only.
hints: Current DoctorReport has fixable_errors/manual_errors/warnings and targetFiles. Runtime status may initially use SSE connected state plus static server info; /api/status can be a future backend task. || packages/dashboard/src/views/doctor.tsx;packages/dashboard/src/api/client.ts;packages/server/src/http.ts
execution_directives: planning only; later implementation should assert no Web command execution
findings: Health absorbs Boundary/Runtime rather than creating a fifth top-level theme.
acceptance_met: Health scope and control-plane boundary specified.

title: Define migration and implementation handoff for Gemini
